content.text:
Great — I have enough context from the panel specs you gave me (192×192mm, 14mm thick) and standard component dimensions. Before I start modeling, I need to nail down a few key things so the design is actually functional and clean:

---

## 🔍 Clarification Questions

**1. Which HUB75 hat exactly?**
Is it the **Adafruit RGB Matrix Bonnet** (fits on top of Pi, ~65×56mm) or the **Electrodragon/Sensehat-style** standalone board? This affects stacking height and mounting.

**2. Power supply — what do you have?**
- A **5V brick adapter** (like a laptop charger) — just needs a cable pass-through?
- A **bare PSU PCB** (like a Mean Well IRM-30-5) — needs a housing/mounting?
- Or a **standard 5V/4A wall adapter** you just want tucked behind the stand?

**3. Desk footprint preference:**
- **Portrait** stand (panel upright, tall) — like a picture frame
- **Landscape** stand (panel horizontal, wide) — like a monitor
- **Angled display** (tilted back ~10-15°) for comfortable desk viewing?

**4. Electronics placement:**
- All hidden **behind the panel** (compact, clean front) — Pi + HAT mount on rear bracket
- Or a **side bay** for easy access to Pi's ports?

**5. 3D printing or machining?**
This affects wall thickness, tolerances, and whether I use snap-fits vs screwed joints.

---

### What I already know:
- **Panel:** 192×192mm face, 14mm deep, HUB75 connector on rear
- **Pi 4B:** 85×56mm PCB, ~30mm tall with HAT stacked
- **Stand approach:** Frame that cradles the panel + rear bracket for electronics

Please NOTE there is a board design problem from around 2025 Feburary until 2025 April. Please see explanation here.
MPC1119 is the version applied the quick fix on old stock, no problem to use, but has an external wire.
MPC1120 is the new fullly fixed board, without any external wires or fix.
The new version of RGB matrix panel drive board, the first version please
find here.
This drive or interface board allow raspberry pi to drive most sizes of RGB matrix panel, software can check
hzeller repository,
code by C or python. You can build very large indoor or outdoor screen and control by RPI.
This new version has following changes MPC1119-V1:
Removed the DS1302 RTC and EEPROM functions and circuits, which are not used by most customers.
Changed to angled connectors to lower the total height, so it can be fit into some enclosures, as customer requested. This is experimental, the connector also block the installation holes underneath, and outer of the PCB.
Added two channels of power supply, one channel of input power supply 5Vwith over-voltage and over-current protection, and same one channel of output power supply 5V.
Package included the 8.5mm connectors, but not soldered for the height reason.
Features:
Reserved 2*3 pin holes: 5V/GND/3V3
Reserved 1*2 pin holes: ROW_E to GND pin holes, default pull up.
Reserved free GPIOs: ID_SD ID_SC pins
Reserved 2*4 pin holes: E_BUFF / P4 / P8 / GND pins.
Reserved GPIOs:
Reserved GPIOs pins for the chain 3 last chip.
You can use the pins directly on the chain 3 hub75 output at logic 5V
You can also remove the last chain 3 74HC245 logic convert chip, and use pins directly from raspberry pi 3.3V logic
Any other suggestions please email us, we can continuously improve this.
Documentation, demos, more silimilar products please see on this page.

content.text:
Good — that's likely the Electrodragon/sengrate board. Since exact PCB dimensions for this HAT aren't publicly documented well, let me ask the remaining focused questions so I can design something accurate for you:

---

## Quick follow-up questions (I just need these 4):

**1. Power supply — which do you have?**

- A bare 5V PSU board?

Overview
Introduction
This product is a 64 × 64 full-color LED matrix panel with 4096 RGB LEDs onboard and 3mm pitch, which supports Raspberry Pi and Arduino, etc. It provides supporting open-source demos and tutorials, suitable for makers or electronics Enthusiasts to start learning, or DIY secondary development into other desktop or wall-mounted display applications.
More
Features
4096 individual RGB LEDs, full-color display, adjustable brightness.
64 × 64 pixels, 3mm pitch, allows displaying text, colorful images, or animation.
192 × 192mm dimensions, moderate size, suitable for DIY desktop display or wall mount display.
Onboard two HUB75 headers, one for controller data input, one for output, and chain support.
Comes with online open-source development resources and tutorials, for use with Raspberry Pi, Arduino, and so on.

content.text: Good. Now building the front bezel frame — the outer shell with a window opening for the panel:

input.script:
import math

# All dims in cm
panel_w  = 19.2
panel_h  = 19.2
panel_d  = 1.4
bezel    = 1.0    # border around panel
wall     = 0.3    # wall thickness
lip_w    = 0.2    # front lip width (holds panel from falling forward)
lip_h    = 0.15   # front lip depth
ledge_d  = 0.4    # rear ledge depth panel rests against

frame_w  = panel_w + bezel * 2   # 21.2 cm
frame_h  = panel_h + bezel * 2   # 21.2 cm
frame_d  = wall + panel_d + ledge_d  # front wall + panel + rear ledge

# ── 1. Outer frame box ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sketch_outer = rootComp.sketches.add(rootComp.xYConstructionPlane)
sketch_outer.name = "FrameOuter"
sketch_outer.sketchCurves.sketchLines.addTwoPointRectangle(
    adsk.core.Point3D.create(0, 0, 0),
    adsk.core.Point3D.create(frame_w, frame_h, 0)
)
prof = sketch_outer.profiles.item(0)
ext_in = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.createInput(
    prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation
)
ext_in.setDistanceExtent(False, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(frame_d))
frame_ext = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.add(ext_in)
frame_body = frame_ext.bodies.item(0)
frame_body.name = "Frame_Outer"

# ── 2. Cut the panel window (opening for panel to sit in from rear) ──────────
# The window is panel_w x panel_h, centered in the frame
# It goes from the front lip inward (leaves lip_h at front, ledge_d at rear)
win_x0 = bezel
win_y0 = bezel
win_x1 = bezel + panel_w
win_y1 = bezel + panel_h

sketch_win = rootComp.sketches.add(rootComp.xYConstructionPlane)
sketch_win.name = "PanelWindow"
sketch_win.sketchCurves.sketchLines.addTwoPointRectangle(
    adsk.core.Point3D.create(win_x0, win_y0, 0),
    adsk.core.Point3D.create(win_x1, win_y1, 0)
)
prof_win = sketch_win.profiles.item(0)
cut_in = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.createInput(

import math

# Frame: X=0..21.2, Y=0..21.2, Z=0..2.1 (front face at Z=0, back at Z=2.1)
# Rear plate: Z=2.1..3.5
# 
# FEET DESIGN:
# - Two feet, one on each side (left and right), like monitor feet
# - Each foot: 30mm wide (X), 80mm deep (Y), wedge shape in Z
# - Foot sits BELOW the frame bottom edge (Y=0 side)
# - The frame sits ON TOP of the feet, tilted 10° back
# - Feet are wedges: thin at front (Y=large, viewer side), tall at back (Y=0, back of desk)
# 
# Approach: extrude foot profile on YZ plane, then position
# Profile on YZ plane: Y=depth axis, Z=height axis
# Wedge: front (Y=8cm from frame back) is thin, back (Y=0) is tall
#
# Actually simplest: sketch foot profile on XZ plane, extrude in Y (width)
# Then position two of them

frame_w    = 21.2   # X
frame_d_y  = 21.2   # Y  
frame_z    = 2.1    # frame top Z (front face is Z=0, back is Z=2.1)

foot_width = 3.0    # 30mm X width of each foot
foot_depth = 9.0    # 90mm Y depth of foot (sticks out from back of frame)
tilt_deg   = 10.0
tilt_rad   = math.radians(tilt_deg)

# The foot supports the frame which is tilted 10° back
# Foot back edge (Y=0) needs to be tall enough to tilt frame
# Foot front edge (Y=foot_depth from back) is at desk level
# Height at back  = foot_depth * tan(tilt) = 9 * tan(10°) = ~1.59 cm
# Height at front = 0.5 cm (small riser so frame front clears desk)
h_front = 0.5   # 5mm front height
h_back  = foot_depth * math.tan(tilt_rad) + h_front  # ~2.09 cm

# Sketch foot profile on XZ plane (depth=X, height=Z)
# Wedge: p1=(0,0), p2=(foot_depth,0), p3=(foot_depth,h_front), p4=(0,h_back)
sk_foot = rootComp.sketches.add(rootComp.xZConstructionPlane)
sk_foot.name = "FootWedge"
lines = sk_foot.sketchCurves.sketchLines
p1 = adsk.core.Point3D.create(0,          0, 0)
p2 = adsk.core.Point3D.create(foot_depth, 0, 0)
p3 = adsk.core.Point3D.create(foot_depth, 0, h_front)
p4 = adsk.core.Point3D.create(0,          0, h_back)
lines.addByTwoPoints(p1, p2)
lines.addByTwoPoints(p2, p3)
lines.addByTwoPoints(p3, p4)
lines.addByTwoPoints(p4, p1)

prof = sk_foot.profiles.item(0)

# Extrude foot_width in Y direction → this creates one foot along left edge
ext_in = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.createInput(
    prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation
)
ext_in.setDistanceExtent(False, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(foot_width))
foot_ext = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.add(ext_in)
foot_L = foot_ext.bodies.item(0)
foot_L.name = "Foot_Left"

# The foot is now at X=0..9, Y=0..3, Z=0..~2.09
# We need to MOVE it so:
#   - X: left side of frame, so X=0..3 → need to rotate 90° around Z, then translate
#   - Y: foot depth goes FROM back of frame (Y=frame_d_y=21.2) OUTWARD (+Y direction)
#   - Z: foot bottom sits at Z=0 (desk), top of foot connects to frame bottom

# Currently: X=0..9 (depth), Y=0..3 (width), Z=0..h_back
# Target:    X=0..3 (width at left), Y=21.2..21.2+9 (depth sticking back), Z=0..h_back
# Transform: swap X↔Y, translate Y by +frame_d_y

moveFeatures = rootComp.features.moveFeatures

# Step 1: Rotate 90° around Z so X(depth) becomes Y
coll = adsk.core.ObjectCollection.create()
coll.add(foot_L)
rot = adsk.core.Matrix3D.create()

import math

# ── FEET — built directly in correct world position ──────────────────────────
# Frame sits at: X=0..21.2, Y=0..21.2, Z=0..2.1
# 
# DESIGN: Two feet on left and right, extending in the +Y direction (out the back of the desk stand)
# Each foot is a wedge:
#   - Sits on the desk (Z=0 at bottom)
#   - Connects to the bottom of the frame at Y=0 (front) and supports it tilted back
#   - The frame leans BACK (toward +Y), so the foot is taller at the BACK (+Y end)
#   - Front of foot (Y=0): height = 0.5 cm  
#   - Back of foot  (Y=foot_depth): height = h_back
#   - Top of foot slopes at 10° to match the frame tilt
#
# Foot dimensions:
foot_w    = 3.0   # 30mm wide in X
foot_d    = 9.0   # 90mm deep in Y
tilt_deg  = 10.0
tilt_rad  = math.radians(tilt_deg)
h_front   = 0.5                                         # 5mm at Y=0
h_back    = h_front + foot_d * math.tan(tilt_rad)      # ~2.09cm at Y=foot_d
wall_t    = 0.35  # 3.5mm wall for FDM

# LEFT FOOT: X = 0 .. foot_w, Y = 0 .. foot_d
# Profile on the XZ plane at Y=0, extruded in +Y
# Use YZ plane sketch so depth goes in Y, height in Z

# Sketch foot side-profile on a plane at X=0 (YZ plane)
sk_L = rootComp.sketches.add(rootComp.yZConstructionPlane)
sk_L.name = "LeftFootProfile"
lines = sk_L.sketchCurves.sketchLines
# In YZ sketch: sketch-X = world-Y (depth), sketch-Y = world-Z (height)
# Profile: bottom flat at Z=0, sloped top
p1 = adsk.core.Point3D.create(0,       0, 0)       # front-bottom  (Y=0, Z=0)
p2 = adsk.core.Point3D.create(foot_d,  0, 0)       # back-bottom   (Y=foot_d, Z=0)
p3 = adsk.core.Point3D.create(foot_d,  0, h_back)  # back-top      (Y=foot_d, Z=h_back)
p4 = adsk.core.Point3D.create(0,       0, h_front) # front-top     (Y=0, Z=h_front)
lines.addByTwoPoints(p1, p2)
lines.addByTwoPoints(p2, p3)
lines.addByTwoPoints(p3, p4)
lines.addByTwoPoints(p4, p1)

prof_L = sk_L.profiles.item(0)
ext_in = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.createInput(
    prof_L, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation
)
# Extrude foot_w in +X direction
ext_in.setDistanceExtent(False, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(foot_w))

# Mirror left foot across X = 21.2/2 = 10.6
mp_input = rootComp.constructionPlanes.createInput()
mp_input.setByOffset(
    rootComp.yZConstructionPlane,
    adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(21.2 / 2)
)
mid_plane = rootComp.constructionPlanes.add(mp_input)

mir_feats = rootComp.features.mirrorFeatures
mir_coll = adsk.core.ObjectCollection.create()

foot_R.name = "Foot_Right"

    b = rootComp.bRepBodies.item(i)
    bb = b.boundingBox

import math

# Feet are at: X correct (0..2.1 left, 19.1..21.2 right), 
#              Y=0..3 (should be depth 0..9), Z=-9..0 (should be height 0..2.09)
# Need to rotate each foot 90° around X axis to swap Y and Z
# Rotation 90° around X: Y→Z, Z→-Y
# After rotation: Y=0..3 becomes Z=0..3 (wrong direction but closer)
# Actually need -90° around X: Y→-Z, Z→Y
# After -90°: old Y(0..3) → new Z(-3..0)... still not right
#
# The issue: on YZ plane, sketch-X maps to world-Y, sketch-Y maps to world-Z
# So my p1..p4 with (foot_d in sketch-X, h_back in sketch-Y) created:
#   world Y = foot_d direction ✓, world Z = h_back ✓ — but extrude went in +X not +X
# Wait the bounding shows Z=-9..0. The extrude of foot_w=3 in +X went... 
# Actually the sketch on YZ plane: sketch coords map as sketch-X→world-Y, sketch-Y→world-Z
# The profile runs Y=0..9 (foot_d), Z=0..h_back ✓
# But extrusion of "foot_w=3 in +X" from the YZ plane goes in +X = 0..3
# So X=0..3, Y=0..9, Z=0..h_back is what I'd expect — but got X=0..2.1, Y=0..3, Z=-9..0
# 
# The extrusion direction from YZ plane is +X (positive). The foot_w=3cm extrude 
# should give X=0..3. But we see X=0..2.1 which = h_back. 
# So the extrude went in Z direction not X — means sketch Y mapped to extrude axis.
# Conclusion: on YZ plane, the extrude goes in the X direction but the sketch 
# coords are: sketch-X = world-Z (not Y!), sketch-Y = world-Y
# 
# Easiest fix: just rotate the foot bodies into the right orientation with a move

moveFeatures = rootComp.features.moveFeatures

for fname in ["Foot_Left", "Foot_Right"]:
    foot = rootComp.bRepBodies.itemByName(fname)
    if foot is None:
        print(f"Not found: {fname}")
        continue
    
    bb = foot.boundingBox
    # Current: X=0..2.1(h_back), Y=0..3(foot_w), Z=-9..0(foot_d, negative)
    # Target:  X=left/right 3cm wide, Y=0..9(depth), Z=0..h_back(height)
    # 
    # Step 1: Rotate -90° around Y axis (X→Z, Z→-X) → X=0..9, Y=0..3, Z=0..2.1
    # Then rotate -90° around Z axis to swap X/Y
    # 
    # Simpler: rotate 90° around Y at origin
    coll = adsk.core.ObjectCollection.create()
    coll.add(foot)
    rot1 = adsk.core.Matrix3D.create()
    rot1.setToRotation(math.pi/2, adsk.core.Vector3D.create(0,1,0), adsk.core.Point3D.create(0,0,0))
    moveFeatures.add(moveFeatures.createInput(coll, rot1))
    
    bb2 = foot.boundingBox
    print(f"After rot1 {fname}: X={bb2.minPoint.x:.1f}..{bb2.maxPoint.x:.1f} "
          f"Y={bb2.minPoint.y:.1f}..{bb2.maxPoint.y:.1f} "
          f"Z={bb2.minPoint.z:.1f}..{bb2.maxPoint.z:.1f}")

print("Done")

import math

# Add fillets to the front bezel edges of Frame_Outer for a clean print-friendly look
# Fillet the 4 outer vertical edges and the front face perimeter edges

frame_body = rootComp.bRepBodies.itemByName("Frame_Outer")

# Find the outer vertical edges (run full height Z=0..2.1)

outer_edges = adsk.core.ObjectCollection.create()
corner_edges = adsk.core.ObjectCollection.create()

for i in range(frame_body.edges.count):
    edge = frame_body.edges.item(i)
    bb = edge.boundingBox
    length = math.sqrt(
        (bb.maxPoint.x - bb.minPoint.x)**2 +
        (bb.maxPoint.y - bb.minPoint.y)**2 +
        (bb.maxPoint.z - bb.minPoint.z)**2
    )
    # Vertical edges at frame corners: full height ~2.1cm
    if length > 1.9 and length < 2.2:
        # Check it's at a corner (x at 0 or 21.2, y at 0 or 21.2)
        cx = (bb.minPoint.x + bb.maxPoint.x) / 2
        cy = (bb.minPoint.y + bb.maxPoint.y) / 2
        if (cx < 0.5 or cx > 20.7) and (cy < 0.5 or cy > 20.7):
            corner_edges.add(edge)

print(f"Corner vertical edges found: {corner_edges.count}")

if corner_edges.count > 0:
    fillet_input = rootComp.features.filletFeatures.createInput()
    fillet_input.addConstantRadiusEdgeSet(
        corner_edges,
        adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(0.3),  # 3mm fillet
        True
    )
    rootComp.features.filletFeatures.add(fillet_input)
    print("Fillets added to outer frame corners")

# Also fillet the foot top edges
for fname in ["Foot_Left", "Foot_Right"]:
    foot = rootComp.bRepBodies.itemByName(fname)
    top_edges = adsk.core.ObjectCollection.create()
    for i in range(foot.edges.count):

        if abs(bb.minPoint.z - 2.0) < 0.05 and abs(bb.maxPoint.z - 2.0) < 0.05:

        fi = rootComp.features.filletFeatures.createInput()

input.script:
import math

# Delete current feet and rebuild as proper bottom-extending legs
for i in range(rootComp.bRepBodies.count - 1, -1, -1):
    b = rootComp.bRepBodies.item(i)
    if "Foot" in b.name:
        b.deleteMe()

print(f"Bodies after cleanup: {rootComp.bRepBodies.count}")

# ── NEW FOOT DESIGN ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Think of it like a monitor stand:
# The frame face is the XZ plane (X=width, Z=height)
# The frame depth is Y direction
# 
# Feet should extend along the BOTTOM of the frame in the Y direction (front-to-back)
# Each foot:
#   - Connects at the bottom of the frame (Z=0)
#   - Spans the full Y depth of the frame (Y=0..21.2) PLUS sticks out the back (Y=21.2..27)
#   - Is 3cm wide in X (left or right)
#   - Is 1.5cm tall (flat slab foot)
#
# This gives the "T" or "monitor leg" look — feet go under and behind frame

frame_w = 21.2
frame_d = 21.2   # Y depth
foot_w  = 3.0    # 30mm wide each foot  
foot_ext = 6.0   # 60mm extension beyond frame back
foot_h  = 1.2    # 12mm tall foot slab (sits flat on desk under frame)

# Left foot: X=0..3, Y=0..27.2, Z=-1.2..0 (below frame bottom face)
sk_footL = rootComp.sketches.add(rootComp.xYConstructionPlane)
sk_footL.name = "FootLeft"
sk_footL.sketchCurves.sketchLines.addTwoPointRectangle(
    adsk.core.Point3D.create(0,              0, 0),
    adsk.core.Point3D.create(foot_w,         frame_d + foot_ext, 0)
)
prof = sk_footL.profiles.item(0)
ext_in = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.createInput(
    prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation
)
# Extrude DOWNWARD (negative Z) since frame sits at Z=0..2.1
ext_in.setOneSideExtent(
    adsk.fusion.DistanceExtentDefinition.create(adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(foot_h)),
    adsk.fusion.ExtentDirections.NegativeExtentDirection
)
ext_L = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.add(ext_in)
foot_L = ext_L.bodies.item(0)
foot_L.name = "Foot_Left"

# Right foot: X=18.2..21.2
sk_footR = rootComp.sketches.add(rootComp.xYConstructionPlane)
sk_footR.name = "FootRight"
sk_footR.sketchCurves.sketchLines.addTwoPointRectangle(
    adsk.core.Point3D.create(frame_w - foot_w, 0, 0),
    adsk.core.Point3D.create(frame_w,          frame_d + foot_ext, 0)
)
prof_R = sk_footR.profiles.item(0)
ext_in2 = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.createInput(
    prof_R, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation
)
ext_in2.setOneSideExtent(
    adsk.fusion.DistanceExtentDefinition.create(adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(foot_h)),
    adsk.fusion.ExtentDirections.NegativeExtentDirection
)
ext_R = rootComp.features.extrudeFeatures.add(ext_in2)
foot_R = ext_R.bodies.item(0)
foot_R.name = "Foot_Right"

# Fillet front and back ends of feet for clean look
for fname in ["Foot_Left", "Foot_Right"]:
    foot = rootComp.bRepBodies.itemByName(fname)
    end_edges = adsk.core.ObjectCollection.create()
    for i in range(foot.edges.count):
        edge = foot.edges.item(i)
        bb_e = edge.boundingBox
        length = math.sqrt(
            (bb_e.maxPoint.x-bb_e.minPoint.x)**2 +
            (bb_e.maxPoint.y-bb_e.minPoint.y)**2 +
            (bb_e.maxPoint.z-bb_e.minPoint.z)**2)
        # Bottom face edges running in X (short edges, length ~3cm) at Z=-1.2
        if abs(bb_e.minPoint.z + 1.2) < 0.1 and abs(bb_e.maxPoint.z + 1.2) < 0.1:
            end_edges.add(edge)
    if end_edges.count > 0:
        fi = rootComp.features.filletFeatures.createInput()
        fi.addConstantRadiusEdgeSet(end_edges, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(0.4), True)
        rootComp.features.filletFeatures.add(fi)

print(f"Final bodies: {rootComp.bRepBodies.count}")
for i in range(rootComp.bRepBodies.count):
    b = rootComp.bRepBodies.item(i)
    bb = b.boundingBox
    print(f"  {b.name}: X={bb.minPoint.x:.1f}..{bb.maxPoint.x:.1f} "
          f"Y={bb.minPoint.y:.1f}..{bb.maxPoint.y:.1f} "
          f"Z={bb.minPoint.z:.2f}..{bb.maxPoint.z:.2f}")

## ✅ LED Matrix Desk Stand — Design Complete

### Parts (4 solid bodies, all exported to STL):

| Part | Dimensions | Print notes |

| **Foot_Right** | 30×272×12mm | Right slab leg (mirror of left) |

### Key features:
- **Panel retention:** 10mm bezel with 2mm front lip + 4× M4 through-holes at 8mm from corners for secure panel mounting
- **Pi 4B mount:** 4× standoff bosses on rear plate, M2.5 threaded holes, centered horizontally in lower half
- **Fan mount:** 60mm cutout + 4× M4 holes at 50mm spacing (Noctua NF-A6x25 compatible), positioned upper-right of rear plate to blow directly over Pi+HAT
- **Power cable:** 20×6mm slot at bottom center of rear plate for 5V cable pass-through
- **3mm corner fillets** on frame for clean FDM printing and desk aesthetics

### STL files saved to:
`~/Documents/Fusion360MCP_Exports/`

### What to print:
- Frame + RearPlate in **PETG or PLA+**, 3+ perimeters, 20% infill
- Feet in PLA, 3 perimeters, 15% infill
- Use **M4×8 countersunk screws** to mount the panel from the rear
- Use **M2.5×6 screws + nuts** for Pi standoffs
